paper-001.title: Attention Mechanisms in Frobnication Models
paper-001.abstract: We study frobnication with attention and report a 7.2 point BLEU gain on the FROB-2020 benchmark.

paragraphs: Frobnication has emerged as a key task.
Prior work used recurrent frobnicators without attention.
Our model applies dot-product attention over frobnication states, trained on 3 million examples.

qas.question: What benchmark do the authors evaluate on?

evidence: report a 7.2 point BLEU gain on the FROB-2020 benchmark
Our model applies dot-product attention over frobnication states

qas.question: What is the model's parameter count?

paper-002.title: A Survey of Debobulation
paper-002.abstract: We survey debobulation methods across 40 papers.

paragraphs: We include papers from 2015 to 2020, categorized into three families.

qas.question: How are the surveyed methods categorized?
